pattern: The active player at any game is just the starting player XORed with the total number of role-swap events seen so far, so a single running flag toggled by two independent triggers (odd score, every 6th game) tracks who is active without ever needing to look back at prior games.
hints: Track which player is currently active with one flag that starts at player one, and update it as you sweep through the games left to right — you never need to revisit an earlier game once you've scored it.

Two separate conditions can each flip the active flag on the same game: the score at this index being odd, and this being every 6th game (0-indexed positions 5, 11, 17, ...). Apply both checks to the same flag for the same index — if both happen to trigger, they cancel out.

After resolving both possible flips for game i, add nums[i] to whichever player's total the (now updated) flag points to, then move on. At the end, subtract the second player's total from the first player's total.

edge_inputs.description: single even score — no swap ever triggers, it all goes to player one
exactly six all-even scores — only the every-6th-game rule fires (at the last index), no odd-score swaps at all
twelve consecutive scores 1..12 — exercises both the odd-score swap and two separate every-6th-game swaps (at indices 5 and 11) interacting
all-odd scores of length 6 — the odd swap and the 6th-game swap coincide on the last game and must cancel, not compound
max length (1000), every score odd and minimal

stress.description: random-length arrays of random scores, seeded, covering many odd/even and 6th-game interaction patterns
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def scoreDifference(self, nums):
        # Independent approach: accumulate a running COUNT of flip events (odd
        # score, every-6th-game) rather than toggling a flag, and derive the
        # active player from the parity of that running count.
        n = len(nums)
        flips = 0
        scores = [0, 0]
        for i in range(n):
            odd_flip = nums[i] % 2
            sixth_flip = 1 if (i + 1) % 6 == 0 else 0
            flips += odd_flip + sixth_flip
            active = flips % 2
            scores[active] += nums[i]
        return scores[0] - scores[1]
